title: Directional Accuracy: Getting the Trend Right
concept: Evaluating whether we correctly predicted 'Up' or 'Down', even if the exact predicted number is slightly off.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why is a naive forecast benchmark important when building time series models?

options.text: It provides a simple baseline to ensure our complex model actually adds predictive value.
It is the only forecasting method allowed in production.
It automatically corrects noise in the data.

exercises.explain: Correct! A naive benchmark is a sanity check to verify if our complex models are actually helpful.

cards.title: Winning the direction
cards.body: Imagine you forecast stock prices. If you predict a stock will go up by $10, and it actually goes up by $1, you made money! But if you predict it goes up by $10 and it goes down by $10, your error was the same distance, but you lost money. Getting the direction right is critical!

cards.title: What is Directional Accuracy?
cards.body: Directional Accuracy measures the percentage of times a model correctly predicts whether the time series will move Up or Down compared to the previous period. It turns a numeric forecasting evaluation into a simple binary choice (Up vs. Down).

cards.title: Predicting the winner vs. the score
cards.body: In sports, you can try to predict the exact final score (regression). Or you can simply predict who wins or loses (classification). Directional accuracy is like predicting if a team wins or loses. Even if you don't get the exact score right, knowing the winner is often what matters most.

cards.title: Daily temperature shifts
cards.body:
A heating company wants to know if they need to turn on city boilers tomorrow:
• Actual temp today: 50°F.
• Forecast tomorrow: 40°F (Down).
• Actual tomorrow: 42°F (Down).
The model was off by 2 degrees, but it got the direction (Down) right. The company turned on the boilers, keeping citizens warm.

cards.title: Low distance error is not everything
cards.body:
Myth: If a model has a low Mean Absolute Error (MAE), it must be excellent at predicting direction.
Reality: A model can stay close to the actual values on average but consistently miss the sudden turnarounds, leading to poor directional accuracy. You need to check both the magnitude of the error and the direction!

cards.title: Distance vs. Direction
cards.body: Comparing distance metrics and directional metrics:

left.text:
Measures the size of the gap.
Concerned with exact values.
Misses the direction of the trend.

right.text:
Measures if the trend direction is correct.
Concerned with Up vs. Down.
Critical for decision making.

exercises.prompt: What does Directional Accuracy measure in time series forecasting?

options.text: The percentage of times the model correctly predicts whether the value will increase or decrease.
The average distance between the predicted number and the actual number.
The speed at which the model generates forecasts.
The total number of seasonal cycles in the dataset.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Directional accuracy checks if the model gets the trend direction (Up or Down) correct.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A model can have a very low average distance error (MAE) but still have poor directional accuracy.
exercises.explain: True. A model can stay close to the values on average but consistently fail to predict whether the trend is reversing.

exercises.sentence: While MAE measures the size of the prediction errors, directional accuracy measures if the model correctly predicts the ___ of the movement.
exercises.explain: Directional accuracy focuses solely on whether the predicted trend movement is in the correct direction (Up or Down).

exercises.prompt: In which scenario is directional accuracy the most critical evaluation metric?

options.text: An automated trading bot deciding whether to buy or sell a stock based on whether it will rise or fall tomorrow.
A factory calculating the exact gallons of water needed to cool a machine.
A warehouse estimating the exact height of storage boxes.

exercises.explain: For buying or selling decisions, knowing the trend direction is the primary key to profit.

exercises.prompt: You are comparing two models for cryptocurrency prices. Model A has an average error of $100 but predicts the correct up/down direction 85% of the time. Model B has an average error of $80 but predicts the correct direction only 45% of the time. Which model is better for a trader who makes buy/sell decisions?

options.text: Model A, because it has high directional accuracy, meaning the trader will make the correct buy/sell decisions most of the time despite slightly larger size errors.
Model B, because it has a lower average error, which guarantees it always gets the direction right.
Neither, because time series models cannot be evaluated using direction.
Model B, because 45% is higher than 85% when predicting prices.

exercises.explain: Correct! In trading, getting the direction right matters most. Model A will guide correct trading actions far more often than Model B, despite having a larger average numeric error.
